t_ch: 有什么事吗？不妨有话直说吧。
t_en: ...Can I help you? If you've got something to say, say it already.

t_ch: 哎，没什么事，就是关心关心你。我早说了，雅利洛-Ⅵ这案子一看就带刺，还榨不出多少油水，何必掺和呢？
t_en: Oh, nothing important — just checking in on you. I told you Jarilo-VI would be a high-risk low-reward case. Why'd you even bother?

t_ch: 做我们这行，心地不能太善良，否则，路会越走越窄的……
t_en: In our line of work, having a kind heart can be more of a liability than an asset. If you're not careful, you'll end up leading yourself down a dead-end road...

t_ch: …还有事吗？我要挂了。
t_en: ...Anything else? If not, I'm hanging up.

t_ch: …哎，别挂别挂！你是真不在聊天的状态啊，行，那说点你会感兴趣的——
t_en: W—Wait, hold on a minute, don't hang up. I get it, you're not in the mood for this conversation right now... Okay, how about this — something that might interest you...

t_ch: 你在雅利洛-Ⅵ玩朋友游戏的时候，「钻石」搞定了筑材物流部的大佬。缺席今天的「圆桌会议」，就是因为这个。
t_en: While you were enjoying your playtime on Jarilo-VI, Diamond was busy taking care of the head honcho of the Building Material Logistics Department. That's why he didn't attend today's meeting.

t_ch: 塔拉梵？
t_en: Taravan?

t_ch: 对，大名鼎鼎的塔拉梵，七人董事会的一员！他那一票要押咱们身上了，明白吗，是咱们，不是市场开拓部，这回奥斯瓦尔多终于笑不出来咯！
t_en: That's right, the renowned Taravan — one of the seven board members! He's throwing his support behind us instead of the Marketing Development Department for once. Ha! Oswaldo won't be laughing anymore!

t_ch: 不过…托帕，「钻石」也算是你我的贵人，如此重要的日子，你来这么一下，多少让战略投资部有些不好交代吧？
t_en: Diamond's been a great help to both of us. Your antics on such a crucial day can't exactly have painted the Strategic Investment Department in a good light, y'know?

t_ch: 既然你明白，咱们就说正事：我现在在庇尔波因特，有笔关乎整个部门的大生意，我这一趟还缺个靠谱的项目管理——朋友，非你莫属啊。
t_en: Since you get the point, let's get down to business... I'm currently at Pier Point — there's a major deal that could affect the whole department. I just so happen to need a reliable project manager for the job... what do you say, old friend?

t_ch: 有趣，什么案子让你也学会「合作」了？
t_en: Huh, and what prompted the mighty Aventurine to start collaborating with others?

t_ch: 还能是什么，「匹诺康尼」呗。
t_en: Penacony, what else?

t_ch: …什么？
t_en: ...What?

t_ch: 对咯~我喜欢的就是这种反应。
t_en: That's right⁓ Now that's the kind of reaction I was hoping for.

t_ch: 钻石最后选了你？我以为会是「欧泊」…或者「龙晶」…那桩生意，至少得P<unbreak>46</unbreak>以上的人出面。
t_en: Hold on a minute... Diamond picked you for this project? I was expecting someone like Opal or Obsidian to handle it — at least somebody higher than rank P<unbreak>46</unbreak>...

t_ch: 谁知道呢。「家族」的人脑袋都不太正常，想和他们做买卖？我看是没戏。
t_en: Honestly, who knows? "The Family" has some pretty questionable characters. If you ask me, doing business with them is a lost cause.

t_ch: 哎呀，三月，{NICKNAME}！真是稀客，什么风把你们吹来了？
t_en: Well, well, look who we have here! {NICKNAME} and March, paying us a visit. What's the occasion?

t_ch: 有件重要的事需要你的意见。
t_en: We need your opinion on an important matter.

t_ch: …嗯？语气这么严肃，这可不像你啊。是什么重要的事，说来给我听听？
t_en: Uh-oh, sounds like something serious is happening. You'd better fill me in.

t_ch: 将公司讨债一事和布洛妮娅面临的抉择转告给了希露瓦……
t_en: You tell Serval about the IPC debt and the choice facing Bronya...

t_ch: 这到底是怎么…不是，这信息量也太大了吧？！不行，我得缓缓……
t_en: ...What? How is that possible? I need a moment to wrap my head around this...

t_ch: 时间很紧，希露瓦！你有什么想法就告诉我们吧。
t_en: Serval, we're racing against the clock! No time for head-wrapping.

t_ch: …我知道了。如果让我来选…我会签下这份合同。
t_en: ...It's okay, I've thought it through... If it were up to me, I'd... sign the contract.

t_ch: 咦？！我还以为以希露瓦的性格，绝对不会同意这条件呢！
t_en: What!? Hold on, are you serious? I thought you'd be against it for sure!

t_ch: 让你意外啦？但我的理由其实很简单。
t_en: Surprised? My reasons are pretty simple.

t_ch: 签下这份合同，就意味着我们不必再为「活着」发愁了。对于这里的大部分居民，尤其是下层区人来说，单单这一点就足够说服他们了。
t_en: Signing this contract would mean we no longer have to worry about "surviving." For most of the residents here, especially those in the Underworld, that would be more than enough to persuade them.

t_ch: 至于给公司打工——嗐，人总归是要工作的，在哪儿干不是干？要是公司的家伙敢欺负到我们头上，到时候再群起反抗也不迟！
t_en: As for working for the company, pfft, people need to work no matter where they are, right? If the IPC tries to take too many liberties, we can always rise up against them!

t_ch: 话又说回来，我从小就憧憬星空，所以才会爱上科学，投身于对星核的研究。如果有人告诉我，我的愿望立刻就能实现，而且只需要付出一点点代价…那我大概不会犹豫。
t_en: You know, I've always longed for the stars since I was a child, which is why I devoted myself to science and the studying of the Stellaron. If someone told me that I could do whatever research I wanted, with only a small price to pay... I probably wouldn't hesitate.

t_ch: 原来如此，希露瓦是这么想的啊……
t_en: I see... I suppose that makes sense.

t_ch: 明白了，我们会把你的意见记下来的。
t_en: Thanks for your input. I'll make a note of your opinion.

t_ch: 我从小就憧憬星空，所以才会爱上科学，投身于对星核的研究。如果有人告诉我，我的愿望立刻就能实现，而且只需要付出一点点代价…那我大概不会犹豫。
t_en: I've always longed for the stars since I was a child, which is why I devoted myself to science and the studying of the Stellaron. If someone told me that I could do whatever research I wanted with only a small price to pay... I probably wouldn't hesitate.

t_ch: 不知道其他人会怎么看待，但这就是我的真实想法。
t_en: I can't speak for everyone, but this is how I really feel.

t_ch: 又见面了两位，别来无恙？
t_en: We meet again! How have you two been?

t_ch: 呃…{NICKNAME}的表情怎么这么凝重？是有什么心事吗？
t_en: Uh-oh... That look on {NICKNAME}'s face tells me something's wrong. I hope it's nothing too serious.

t_ch: 有件重要的事需要你的意见。
t_en: We need your opinion on an important matter.

t_ch: 看来是很严肃的事啊…请说吧，我会仔细听的。
t_en: Sounds serious. I'm all ears.

t_ch: 将公司讨债一事和布洛妮娅面临的抉择转告给了佩拉……
t_en: You tell Pela about the IPC debt and the choice facing Bronya...

t_ch: 这…你们说的这些都是真实的吗？！不会是编出来捉弄人的故事吧？
t_en: Really...? You better not be making things up!

t_ch: 我们当然也希望是虚构的…可惜这些都是现实。
t_en: I wish it were made up... Unfortunately it's all true.

t_ch: 时间很紧了，佩拉！布洛妮娅需要知道大家的想法。
t_en: We don't have much time, Pela! Bronya wants to get everyone's opinion.

t_ch: 这么重大的抉择，却要在这么短的时间里…冷静，佩拉…冷静地思考。
t_en: Wait, you're telling me I have to make this huge decision on the spot? At least give me a few seconds to think it over.

t_ch: 我…我应该想好了。
t_en: Okay, I think I've got it.

t_ch: 让我决定的话…我可能会同意公司开出的条件。
t_en: If it were up to me... I'd probably agree to the IPC's conditions.

t_ch: 原来如此…佩拉是怎么考虑的呢？
t_en: Huh, okay... Any particular reason?

t_ch: 我看到过几组观测数字，按照现在寒潮消退的速率，至少要再过三四代人的时间，笼罩城市的风雪才能完全停歇。
t_en: I've looked at some of the climate reports, and based on the data I've seen, it'll take three or four more generations for the snow to completely subside.

t_ch: …这还是在没有其它意外发生的前提之下。老实说，比起没办法亲眼见证的未来…我更在乎当下，更关心自己身边的人。
t_en: And that's assuming no other disasters occur. I care more about those in the present than a future I can't be sure of.

t_ch: 如果公司的方案真的能够立竿见影，付出这样的代价在我看来是值得的。
t_en: If the IPC is able to bring about immediate change, then I think that's a price well worth paying.

t_ch: 用双手为后人筑造地基——听上去特别伟大，对吧？但是对于切身参与到这项工程中的人们来说，这里面又有多少乐趣可言呢？
t_en: Building a better world for future generations sound great, right? But is it any fun for the engineers and Architects who are directly involved in such a pursuit?

t_ch: 原来如此，相比冀望缥缈的未来，佩拉更希望身边的人能过上好的生活……
t_en: I see. So compared to a distant and uncertain future, what you really care about is making sure the people around you have a good life...

t_ch: 明白了，我们会把你的意见记下来的。
t_en: Thanks for your input. I'll make a note of your opinion.

t_ch: 如果公司的方案真的能够立竿见影，付出这样的代价在我看来是值得的。
t_en: If the IPC is able to bring about immediate change, then I think that's a price well worth paying.

t_ch: 用双手为后人筑造地基——听上去特别伟大，对吧？但是对于切身参与到这项工程中的人们来说，这里面又有多少乐趣可言呢？
t_en: Building a better world for future generations sound great, right? But is it any fun for the engineers and Architects who are directly involved in such a pursuit?

t_ch: 不知道我的意见能有多少分量…但至少这是我的心声。
t_en: I'm not sure how much weight my opinion carries, but that's my take on the matter.

t_ch: 噢，这不是{NICKNAME}和三月小姐吗！许久不见，没想到你们又来贝洛伯格了。
t_en: Is that {NICKNAME} and Miss March? It's been a while! I didn't think you'd be back in Belobog so soon.

t_ch: 最近城里不太平啊。突然冒出来的黑衣人四处作乱，铁卫拿他们都没什么办法……
t_en: It's been pretty hectic in the city lately. All these people dressed in black suddenly showed up, and even the Silvermane Guards have had trouble dealing with them...

t_ch: 有件重要的事需要你的意见。
t_en: We need your opinion on an important matter.

t_ch: 哦？是什么事，居然需要收集我的意见？
t_en: You need my opinion? Happy to help.

t_ch: 将公司讨债一事和布洛妮娅面临的抉择转告给了邓恩……
t_en: You tell Dunn about the IPC debt and the choice facing Bronya...

t_ch: …原来那帮黑衣人是这个身份！简直不敢相信，真相居然如此骇人！
t_en: ...So that's who they are. Huh, it's worse than I imagined.

t_ch: 守护者大人一定面临着空前巨大的压力…我们真的给不了她任何帮助吗？真是让人……
t_en: The Madam Guardian must be under a lot of pressure... Is there really nothing we can do to assist her?

t_ch: 我们来这儿就是为了替她收集大家的声音。时间不等人，我们想知道你是怎么看的。
t_en: We're here to gather everyone's opinion on the matter. I know this is a little sudden, but we're running out of time. What's your take?

t_ch: …当然是拒绝！绝对不能妥协！
t_en: Why, I'd refuse the contract, of course! There is no room for compromise!

t_ch: 看看那群黑衣人嚣张跋扈的样子，怎么能信任那种家伙？签了合同又如何，谁能保证他们不会毁约？
t_en: Just look at them — arrogant, stomping around here as if they own the place. How can such people be trusted? Who's to say they won't breach the contract?

t_ch: 面对这种胁迫，我们必须反抗、反抗、反抗到底！一秒都不能动了服软的心思！
t_en: Faced with this sort of coercion, we must resist them with all our might! Surrendering is not an option!

t_ch: 一定要提醒守护者大人，不能主动踩进对方设好的陷阱里！
t_en: You must warn the Madam Guardian not to allow herself to fall into their trap!

t_ch: 邓恩，真不愧有一颗军人的心啊……
t_en: A soldier's perspective is always valuable, Dunn.

t_ch: 明白了，我们会把你的意见记下来的。
t_en: Thanks for your input. I'll make a note of your opinion.

t_ch: 面对这种胁迫，我们必须反抗、反抗、反抗到底！一秒都不能动了服软的心思！
t_en: Faced with this sort of coercion, we must resist them with all our might! Surrendering is not an option!

t_ch: 一定要提醒守护者大人，不能主动踩进对方设好的陷阱里！
t_en: You must warn the Madam Guardian not to allow herself to fall into their trap!

t_ch: 上层区该问的人已经都问过了，对吧？
t_en: Did we miss anyone in the Overworld? I think we asked just about everyone we needed to.

t_ch: 从现在收集的结果来看…支持和公司签合同的声音好像要更大一些呢。
t_en: From what I've gathered, it seems like most people in the Overworld are leaning towards signing the IPC's contract.

t_ch: 抓紧时间去下层吧，我很好奇娜塔莎和希儿会怎么看这件事……
t_en: We'd better hurry to the Underworld and see what Natasha and Seele make of it all...

t_ch: 小三月，{NICKNAME}？你们居然在下层区…而且还绷着脸，发生什么了吗？
t_en: Miss March, {NICKNAME}, what brings you two to the Underworld? And why the long face?

t_ch: 有件重要的事需要你的意见。
t_en: We need your opinion on an important matter.

t_ch: 虽然有点突然…但既然两位专程登门造访，想必事态相当紧急。请说吧，我会仔细听的。
t_en: Seems a little out of the blue, but since you came all this way, it must be urgent. Go ahead.

t_ch: 将公司讨债一事和布洛妮娅面临的抉择转告给了娜塔莎……
t_en: You tell Natasha about the IPC debt and the choice facing Bronya...

t_ch: 还真是…密集的信息。抱歉两位，我知道你们在赶时间，但我需要稍微思考一下。
t_en: That's a lot of information to take in. Sorry, both of you — I know you're in a hurry, but I need a moment to think.

t_ch: 别着急，娜塔！一定要把你最真实的想法告诉我们。
t_en: No stress, Nat. Just tell us what you really think.

t_ch: …我已经想好了。如果我是布洛妮娅，就不会签署这份合同。
t_en: I've thought about it, and... Well, if I were Bronya, I wouldn't sign it.

t_ch: 的确，公司开出的价码很诱人。如果拒绝，贝洛伯格不仅要承担还清巨额债务的压力，还会被一方极为强大的势力盯上。
t_en: I won't deny, the offer on the table is a very tempting one... and if we refuse, Belobog will not only have the burden of a huge debt — we may also incite the wrath of a very powerful force...

t_ch: 但即便如此…我想起了上学时读到过的一句话：「愿意放弃自由寻求保障的人，最后两者皆空」。
t_en: ...but even so, a quote I read back in school came to me just now: "Those who are willing to give up freedom for security will end up with neither."

t_ch: 下层区所经历的一切，让我对这句话有了深切的了解。于我个人而言…我宁愿背负巨石在雪原里跋涉，也不愿意被关在漂亮的笼子里，过虚假的生活。
t_en: Living in the Underworld has taught me that this phrase is true. I'd rather trek through the frigid Snow Plains than live in a beautiful cage.

t_ch: 娜塔莎的想法，听上去很有说服力耶……
t_en: ...Wow, that's a pretty convincing argument...

t_ch: 反对票又多了一张——谢谢你的回答，娜塔。
t_en: Another vote for the against pile. Thanks for your insight, Nat.

t_ch: 「愿意放弃自由寻求保障的人，最后两者皆空」。
t_en: "Those who are willing to give up freedom for security will end up with neither."

t_ch: 这个道理，我衷心希望布洛妮娅也能理解。
t_en: I hope Bronya takes these words seriously.

t_ch: 三月姐姐…还有{NICKNAME}{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！你们…你们怎么来了？
t_en: Miss March, {F#big sister}{M#big brother} {NICKNAME}! What brings you to the Underworld?

t_ch: 克拉拉，你好呀！咦？史瓦罗是出门了吗？
t_en: Hey there, Clara! Huh? Is Svarog not around?

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生，和一个叫托帕的姐姐一起走了……
t_en: Mr. Svarog went out for a walk with a lady called Topaz...

t_ch: 什么，托帕？！你知道他们去哪了吗？
t_en: What!? Topaz!? Do you know where they went?

t_ch: 克拉拉也不清楚…但是史瓦罗先生答应我，他很快就会回来的……
t_en: I'm not sure... but Mr. Svarog promised he'd be back soon...

t_ch: 怎么办，{NICKNAME}…本来我还想说，史瓦罗肯定能提出有建设性的意见……
t_en: Ugh! What now, {NICKNAME}? Svarog's super logical, we should definitely get his opinion...

t_ch: 还有克拉拉在呢。
t_en: At least Clara's here.

t_ch: 克拉拉的想法也很重要。
t_en: Clara's opinion is just as important!

t_ch: 也对哦，丹恒以前好像也说过？克拉拉虽然年纪还小，却总能把事情看得很透彻。
t_en: True. Dan Heng's always saying that Clara's really insightful for her age.

t_ch: …欸？三月姐姐，是有什么问题要问克拉拉吗？
t_en: Huh? Miss March, do you want to ask me something?

t_ch: 将公司讨债一事和布洛妮娅面临的抉择转告给了克拉拉……
t_en: You tell Clara about the IPC debt and the choice facing Bronya...

t_ch: 好复杂，好沉重…克拉拉不知道该怎么……
t_en: Uhh... That's complicated... I don't know what to say...

t_ch: 没关系的，克拉拉！你只要把当下真实的想法告诉我们就好。
t_en: It's alright, Clara, no pressure. Just tell us how you feel about it.

t_ch: 那个…克拉拉觉得，如果还有选择余地的话…未来的路，还是应该由大家自己决定。
t_en: I... I think if we have a choice for the future, it should be everyone's decision.

t_ch: 因为…克拉拉、虎克、还有鼹鼠党其他的小朋友们，我们一起玩的时候…聊到过以后的梦想。
t_en: Because... when I was playing with Hook, The Moles, and the other kids... We talked about our dreams...

t_ch: 大家的愿望各不相同，有不同的色彩…如果同意了托帕姐姐的条件，大家是不是就没法实现自己的梦想了？
t_en: Everyone has different dreams. If we accept Miss Topaz's offer, then doesn't that mean our dreams will never come true?

t_ch: 梦想被夺走的感觉…克拉拉不知道，但只是想象一下…一定很不好受吧？
t_en: Just taking away people's dreams like that... I don't know... It feels wrong.

t_ch: 的确，透过孩子们的眼睛来看，这个世界本该有无限多的可能性。
t_en: You're right. Children see the world as a place where anything is possible...

t_ch: 要是人生的方向在这个时候就被定死了…呃，不行！光是想想我就心寒得打颤了！
t_en: ...just the thought of our lives being predestined from birth makes me shiver!

t_ch: 谢谢你，克拉拉！你的意见对我们来说非常重要。
t_en: Thanks, Clara. Your thoughts mean a lot to us.

t_ch: 克拉拉觉得，如果还有选择的余地的话…未来的路，还是应该由大家自己决定。
t_en: I think if we have a choice for the future, it should be everyone's decision.

t_ch: 虎克、尤利安，还有鼹鼠党的其他小朋友们…克拉拉不想看到大家的梦想被夺走。
t_en: Hook, Julian, and The Moles all have different dreams. I don't think anyone should take them away...

t_ch: 哎唷，这不是两位贝洛伯格的大救星吗？我之前就听说你们回来了，还合计你们啥时候能想起我这老头子呢！
t_en: None other than the saviors of Belobog! To what do I owe the pleasure? To be honest, I'm surprised you even remembered an old geezer like me!

t_ch: 不过看你们这脸色…怎么，有心事？
t_en: But... I can tell by the look on your faces you're probably not dropping by to say hi.

t_ch: 有件重要的事需要你的意见。
t_en: We need your opinion on an important matter.

t_ch: 噢…我猜跟之前那群乱来的黑衣人有关系，是吧？
t_en: Let me guess — something to do with these unruly people in black showing up everywhere?

t_ch: 行啊，正好我也对那些家伙的背景好奇着呢。跟我说说吧，看看能不能帮上什么忙。
t_en: Well, ask away! It just so happens I'm curious about them myself. Maybe I can be of some help.

t_ch: 将公司讨债一事和布洛妮娅面临的抉择转告给了奥列格……
t_en: You tell Oleg about the IPC debt and the choice facing Bronya...
I see...

t_ch: …不好办啊，真是不好办。布洛妮娅姑娘年纪轻轻，居然要承受这前无古人的压力……
t_en: ...That's a tricky situation alright... a lot for anyone to handle, let alone someone as young as Lady Bronya.

t_ch: 我们认识的贝洛伯格人里，就属大叔你的人生阅历最丰富了。你觉得该怎么选呢？
t_en: Mr. Oleg, out of all the people we know in Belobog, you have the most life experience. What do you think we should do?

t_ch: 想听实话吗？到了我这年纪，那些宏图大业，或者什么远大的计划，早就没什么吸引力了。
t_en: Wanna hear the truth? I'm afraid great ambitions and lofty plans no longer hold much appeal for an old-timer like me.

t_ch: 如果这个「公司」确实跟你们说的那样神通广大，一夜间就能让这个世界回到千年前的模样——在我眼里，这实在没什么好犹豫的。
t_en: If this "IPC" does indeed possess the magical power to restore Belobog back to its former self overnight, then I think the answer is clear.

t_ch: 不如想想事情的反面。如果拒绝了，接下来会发生什么？重建计划本就一眼看不到头，现在又凭空多出一笔巨债要还，只靠我们自己真的顶得住吗？
t_en: Have you considered the opposite? What about if we refuse? The reconstruction plan already sounds like an endless endeavor. With the added burden of a massive debt... How's that something we can manage on our own?

t_ch: 要说这里唯一让我担心的，就是布洛妮娅姑娘自己。她能不能承受住来自自己人民的非议？她准备好背上可能流传千年的骂名了吗？
t_en: What worries me most is how Lady Bronya will handle the backlash from her people. Will she be able to withstand the criticism? Is she prepared to carry the weight of a tarnished reputation for years to come?

t_ch: 布洛妮娅，「最后的大守护者」…
t_en: Bronya... "The Last Supreme Guardian..."

t_ch: 的确，我还没从这个角度考虑过…真是个沉重的称号啊。
t_en: Huh, I guess I never looked at it from that angle... I wouldn't wish that title on anyone.

t_ch: 我觉得她足够强大。
t_en: I think she can handle a little criticism.

t_ch: 嗯，我也这么觉得。但是「出卖了人民的君主」什么的…一想到以后可能有人这么评价她，我就觉得很难受……
t_en: I think so too. But could you imagine having to hear names like "The Traitorous Ruler" for the rest of your life?

t_ch: 明白了，奥列格大叔，一票赞成。谢谢你提供意见。
t_en: Thank you for your thoughts, Mr. Oleg. Another vote for the "for" pile.

t_ch: 到了我这年纪，那些宏图大业，或者什么远大的计划，早就没什么吸引力了。
t_en: The truth is, great ambitions and lofty plans no longer hold much appeal to an old-timer like me.

t_ch: 如果这个「公司」确实跟你们说的那样神通广大，一夜间就能让这个世界回到千年前的模样——在我眼里，这实在没什么好犹豫的。
t_en: If this "IPC" does indeed possess the magical power to restore Belobog back to its former self overnight, then I think the answer is clear.

t_ch: 但这只是我一己之见。最后的最后，还是得看布洛妮娅姑娘怎么判断。
t_en: But that's just my personal opinion. In the end, it's all up to Lady Bronya.

t_ch: 呼…一、一下跑了这么多地方，我已经开始喘了……
t_en: Whew... All that running around has worn me out.

t_ch: 所以，现在的收集结果是——
t_en: So, the final conclusion is...

t_ch: ——票数居然完全持平啊。怎么办…支持和反对的人都有自己的理由，而且听上去都合情合理……
t_en: ...The votes are completely even! Ugh, I don't know what to do — both sides have really compelling arguments.

t_ch: 还剩下一个人没问。
t_en: There's still one person left to ask.

t_ch: 对耶，还没问希儿呢！
t_en: Right! We haven't asked Seele!

t_ch: 只能由布洛妮娅自己决断了。
t_en: Only Bronya can make the final call.

t_ch: 不对，还漏了一个人！咱们还没去问希儿的意见呢！
t_en: Wait a minute, we forgot to ask Seele! Let's go see what she has to say.

t_ch: 她应该还在大矿区协助矿民吧？走，我们去找她！
t_en: She's probably still in the Great Mine helping the miners. Come on, let's get going!

t_ch: 咦？你俩怎么这么快就又回来了，是有新消息吗？
t_en: Back so soon? Any news?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你脸色不太好啊…出什么问题了吗？
t_en: A—Are you feeling alright, {NICKNAME}? You look a little uneasy.

t_ch: 有件重要的事需要你的意见。
t_en: We need your opinion on an important matter.

t_ch: 重要的事…需要我的意见？
t_en: Important matter, huh...?

t_ch: 听你这语气就感觉不妙。来吧，我做好心理准备了。
t_en: I can tell from your tone this isn't gonna be a lighthearted chat... but I'm ready to hear it.

t_ch: 将公司讨债一事和布洛妮娅面临的抉择转告给了希儿……
t_en: You tell Seele about the IPC debt and the choice facing Bronya...

t_ch: 我…算是勉强听懂了。本来以为只是公司的喽啰又闯了什么祸，没想到……
t_en: I... think I get it. At first, I thought it was just a few IPC ruffians overstepping their boundaries...

t_ch: …没想到居然攸关贝洛伯格的存亡，对吧？
t_en: Yeah, you didn't think the future of Belobog was at stake, right?

t_ch: 嗯，我得想想……
t_en: Right... I need a little time to process this...

t_ch: 现在的结果是平票——你的意见很关键，希儿。
t_en: As it stands, the votes are even. Your opinion will make all the difference.

t_ch: …我想好了。我弃票。
t_en: ...In that case... I... I abstain.
Huh!?

t_ch: 再想想吧，你这一票至关重要…
t_en: Your vote is extremely important...

t_ch: 我已经想清楚了——做选择是布洛妮娅的责任，不是我的。
t_en: I've thought about it... This responsibility rests on Bronya's shoulders, not mine.

t_ch: 你得担起这个责任才行。
t_en: You have to shoulder this responsibility.

t_ch: 不，不对。这是布洛妮娅的责任。
t_en: No, this responsibility rests on Bronya's shoulders, not mine.

t_ch: 同意还是拒绝，不管她现在偏向于选哪个…她肯定比我看得更远，考虑到的东西绝对比我更多，更完整。
t_en: No matter the choice she makes, she has far greater foresight than I do — she can see the bigger picture more clearly than I can.

t_ch: 现在的结果是平票——也就是说，我一时的想法可能会让杠杆倾斜，左右她的判断。
t_en: The vote is tied. If I were to give you my view, it would tip the scales and possibly impact her final choice.

t_ch: 这种事不该由我来做。不管布洛妮娅选择了哪条路，我都会追随…我唯一不能做的就是替她下决定。
t_en: I'll respect whatever decision she makes, but... it's not my place to make it for her.

t_ch: 原来希儿是这么想的…的确，现在的结果恰好说明，人们对这件事没有统一的立场，每个人的看法都完全不同。
t_en: ...Understood, Seele. This is a difficult decision to make, and everyone feels differently about it.

t_ch: 这才是最需要领袖站出来的时候呀……
t_en: It's times like these that a leader must stand up and do what's needed...

t_ch: 嗯。所以我才说，这是布洛妮娅的责任。她是不会推卸的，也不会逃避。我从头到尾都相信她。
t_en: Right... It's Bronya's decision. She knows what's at stake — she won't run away from her duty. I trust her wholeheartedly.

t_ch: 要是她问起来，你们就拿我的原话回复。她肯定能明白的。
t_en: If she asks you, just let her know my reasoning. She'll understand.

t_ch: 结果到了最后，还是得由布洛妮娅自己来做决定啊。
t_en: Well, it's all in Bronya's hands now. She has to make the final call.

t_ch: 她对这个世界太重要了。
t_en: She plays a crucial role on this world.

t_ch: 可不是嘛，还好现在领导贝洛伯格的是她……
t_en: Right? I'm glad Belobog has someone like her in charge...

t_ch: 你相信她的判断吗？
t_en: Do you trust her judgment?

t_ch: …那是当然！布洛妮娅的为人，可是有咱们列车组官方认证的。
t_en: You bet! The Express can vouch for Bronya. That says a lot about her character.

t_ch: 时间刚好，我们快去向她汇报吧？不知道她和克里珀堡的大臣们商议得如何了。
t_en: The time has come — let's go fill her in. I'm curious to see how she's handling those Qlipoth ministers.

t_ch: 三月，{NICKNAME}…你们回来了啊。
t_en: March, {NICKNAME}, you're back.

t_ch: 结果…怎么样？城里的大家是怎么想的？
t_en: So... what's the verdict? What do the people think?

t_ch: 我们把上下层区都跑了个遍，问了很多人的意见。最后的结果是…平票。
t_en: We talked to people in both the Overworld and Underworld. The final result was... a tie.

t_ch: 原来如此…每个人都有自己看问题的角度，这个结果倒也正常。
t_en: I see... I suppose everyone has their own way of seeing things. I can't say I'm surprised.

t_ch: 那…你们问希儿了吗？她是怎么选的？
t_en: ...What was Seele's perspective on the matter?

t_ch: 她说会追随你的选择。
t_en: She's behind you, whatever you decide.

t_ch: 她弃票了。
t_en: She thought it wasn't her place to say.

t_ch: …真像她的作风，我大概能想象出来她是怎么和你们沟通的了。
t_en: ...I should've known. That sounds like Seele alright.

t_ch: 你这边呢，大家有讨论出什么结果吗？办公室里这么嘈杂，我一句话都听不清……
t_en: Did you come to a conclusion with the ministers? It's so loud I can't tell what anyone's saying...

t_ch: 这边的情况也差不多。两边各执一词，根本没法达成共识。
t_en: It's pretty much the same situation here. Neither party is willing to compromise.

t_ch: 该到做决定的时候了。这种…这么混乱的局面，不能再持续下去了。
t_en: The time has to come to make a decision... This chaos cannot be allowed to continue any longer.

t_ch: 等等，杰帕德刚才是不是说了铆钉镇的深处？难道托帕是在……
t_en: Wait a sec, did Gepard say that he's in Rivet Town? Could that mean Topaz is...

t_ch: …不管啦，咱们先去铆钉镇找他汇合吧。
t_en: No time for guesses! Let's just head to Rivet Town and find Gepard first.

t_ch: 铆钉镇——杰帕德应该就在那里等我们，对吧？
t_en: Gepard should be waiting for us in Rivet Town, right?

t_ch: 又要去老地方冒险了，我们走！
t_en: Here we go again, back to our old stomping grounds for another adventure!

t_ch: 大守护者已经将情况告知于我，让我协助列车组的各位找到托帕。
t_en: The supreme guardian has briefed me on the situation and asked that I assist you in locating Topaz.

t_ch: 准备好了就告诉我，我们一起进入铆钉镇。
t_en: Ready to enter Rivet Town? We'll go in together when everyone is set.

t_ch: 准备好了。
t_en: Ready.

t_ch: 不知道前方是否有公司的人阻拦，务必提高警惕。
t_en: Keep your guards up — we might run into IPC agents ahead.

t_ch: 稍等一下。
t_en: Just a minute.

t_ch: 当然，准备工作务必要做周全。
t_en: Take your time — preparation is key.

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生！你还好吗？托帕和公司的人没把你怎么样吧？
t_en: Mr Svarog! Are you alright? I hope Topaz and her IPC henchmen didn't hurt you.

t_ch: 你们到了，外来者。系统功能一切正常，我并未与公司的使者起冲突。
t_en: Greetings, outsiders. System functions are normal — I have had no conflict with the IPC's representatives.

t_ch: 还以为又要和你当对手了。
t_en: I thought we might be facing off against each other for a moment.

t_ch: 那会是最坏的情况。
t_en: Such a result would be highly undesirable.

t_ch: 托帕就在前方。她本可以用手段强制我服从命令，但却并未如此行事。
t_en: Topaz is ahead. She had the means to force me to comply with her orders, but chose not to.

t_ch: 于她看来，将贝洛伯格并入公司是唯一的「存护」之道。我虽持有不同观点，但以现有的算力难以找到反驳的证据。
t_en: From her perspective, the only way to "preserve" Belobog is by incorporating it into the IPC. While I do not agree with her viewpoint, I lack the computational power to provide evidence to the contrary.

t_ch: 尽管几率很低，我仍然希望你们能够避免冲突局面。
t_en: While it is unlikely, I still encourage the avoidance of any potential conflicts.

t_ch: 「几率很低」——史瓦罗先生都这么说了……
t_en: "Unlikely, potential..." meaning there could still be conflicts...

t_ch: 不必慌张，三月。列车组从不提倡冲突，可一旦快进到刀兵相向的场面，我们也从不胆怯。一路都是这么过来的，不是吗？
t_en: Don't worry, March. The Express never pursues conflict as a first means... but if it comes down to it, we won't back down either. That's how it's always been.

t_ch: 的确，姬子说得对。不管结果如何，我们只要做到问心无愧就够了！
t_en: Himeko's right. As long as we can look at ourselves in the mirror and know we did the right thing, that's all that matters.

t_ch: 真不容易呀，能通过和平的方式解决问题真是太好了。
t_en: Phew, I was worried things were gonna get ugly again. A peaceful solution is always the best solution.

t_ch: 话说回来…他们正在修的，莫非就是之前跟我们战斗过的那台大机器人？我有印象，你当时夺取了它的控制权，帮我们打赢了可可利亚！
t_en: Speaking of solutions... Are they repairing that giant robot — the one we fought against? I remember you took control of it and helped us defeat Cocolia!

t_ch: 不知道还能不能过「煦日节」…
t_en: I wonder if we'll have time to celebrate the Solwarm Festival.

t_ch: 对哦，我都快忘了！咱们原本只是想来体验节日气氛的……
t_en: Right! I'd totally forgotten — that's the whole reason we came here in the first place!

t_ch: 城里的大家都在帮忙施工，庆典恐怕是办不起来了。不过往好处想，能和托帕小姐这样的公司高管搭上线，也算是不虚此行了吧？
t_en: Looks like everyone in the city's helping with construction — doubt there'll be much time for celebrating. Still, look on the bright side — we got to meet a top IPC executive! The trip wasn't a complete waste, right?

t_ch: 希望托帕的谈判能有好结果。
t_en: I hope Topaz's negotiations go smoothly.

t_ch: 一定可以的！你不觉得吗？托帕小姐身上有种…唔，该怎么形容呢…有种势在必得的气质！
t_en: Miss Topaz is definitely up to the job! She has that aura of... what's the word... invincibility!

t_ch: 虽然这次下来没能体验到节日庆典的气氛，但是往好处想，能和托帕小姐这样的公司高管搭上线，也算是不虚此行了吧？
t_en: Well, we didn't get to enjoy the festivities this time round, but at least we still got to meet a top IPC executive!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我早听你和三月夸奖过这位大守护者姑娘。但亲眼看到她的行动力和手段，还是让我感到惊讶。
t_en: Y'know, {NICKNAME}, I'd heard you and March speak highly of the Supreme Guardian before, but seeing her methods firsthand left me in awe.

t_ch: …同时我们也该庆幸，雅利洛-Ⅵ这个项目是由托帕小姐接手的。如果换做「战略投资部」的其他人，事情的进展恐怕会更…难以预料。
t_en: We should also be glad that Miss Topaz is the one in charge of the Jarilo-VI project. If it were someone else from the Strategic Investment Department, things might have gotten... complicated.

t_ch: 这是什么意思，姬子？
t_en: What do you mean, Himeko?

t_ch: 「战略投资部」集结了公司内部的精英，他们中的大部分手腕都非常…强硬。
t_en: The Strategic Investment Department comprises the IPC's most elite employees, many of whom can be very... forceful.

t_ch: 我觉得托帕已经很强硬了。
t_en: You mean Topaz didn't make things complicated enough?

t_ch: 那说明你对「战略投资部」这个部门还不太了解，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: That tells me you really don't understand the Strategic Investment Department, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 不过这个话题可以等你回列车再聊。你和小三月又经历了一段不可思议的旅程，最好还是先忘记这些杂事，饱饱地睡上一觉吧。
t_en: Let's talk about this later when you're back on the Express. You and March must be tired after such an incredible journey — it's time you got some well-deserved rest.

t_ch: 放假还要带娃…
t_en: Even when I'm off work, I'm looking after a child...

t_ch: 简直比上班还累…
t_en: It's even more exhausting than working in an office...

t_ch: 妈妈！妈妈！
t_en: Mommy! Mommy!

t_ch: 我要吃太阳薄饼嘛！
t_en: I want suncake!

t_ch: 听说，放完假要调休…
t_en: I hear I have to work overtime for taking time off when I get back...

t_ch: 连上八天，不如杀了我…
t_en: Eight days in a row. Honestly, just kill me...

t_ch: 早知道就留几天假了…
t_en: If I'd known, I'd have saved some of my holiday.

t_ch: 小朋友，快来看看呀！
t_en: Look here, little one!

t_ch: 铁卫体验营，这里报名！
t_en: Silvermane Guard training camp, sign up here!

t_ch: 喔，我要报名！
t_en: Oh, I want to sign up!

t_ch: 先把借的零花钱还我…
t_en: Give me the change you borrowed first...

t_ch: 哪来的黑衣人？
t_en: Where did those people in black come from?

t_ch: 穿的什么奇装异服？
t_en: What's with that weird get up?

t_ch: 行头越齐，德行越差…
t_en: The smarter the uniform, the looser the morals...

t_ch: 我表弟还在里面…
t_en: My cousin's still inside...

t_ch: 应该没什么事吧？
t_en: They'll be okay, right?

t_ch: 他性子急，我怕打起来…
t_en: He's short-tempered, I'm worried he could start a fight...

t_ch: 大伯，没事吧？
t_en: Are you okay, sir?

t_ch: 没啥事…就有点儿晕…
t_en: I'm fine... Just a little dizzy...

t_ch: 怎么又撞上倒霉事了…
t_en: How have I ended up in a spot like this again...

t_ch: 这地儿是不是风水不行…
t_en: Is this place cursed or something...

t_ch: 这又是在闹哪样…
t_en: What's going on now...

t_ch: 算了，权当摸鱼…
t_en: Forget it, they're just slacking on the job...

t_ch: 那黑衣人还塞钱给我…
t_en: That person in black even palmed me some money...

t_ch: 我怎么没这好运？
t_en: How come I never get this lucky?

t_ch: 托帕小姐，你刚才说的追责……
t_en: Miss Topaz, you mentioned accountability...

t_ch: 别担心，没什么我搞不定的~职级什么的都不重要，找到解决问题的最佳方案才是硬道理。
t_en: Don't worry, it's nothing I can't handle. There are more important things than titles and ranks — finding the best solution is what matters most.

t_ch: 会扣工资吗？
t_en: Will they cut your pay?

t_ch: 扣就扣吧~反正我早就过了纯为挣钱而工作的那个阶段了。
t_en: If that's what they want to do, then so be it. I've long passed the stage of working just for money.

t_ch: 不会被开除吧…
t_en: Won't you get fired?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，怎么可能，最多降我一两个职级，再扣点奖金。别担心，我现在工作可不是纯为了赚钱~
t_en: Haha, that's unlikely. At most, they might lower my rank and reduce my bonus a bit. But don't worry, I work for far more than just a paycheck.

t_ch: 也辛苦你了，{NICKNAME}。真没想到会再次把你们卷进危机里…还差点导致列车跟公司之间的关系破裂。
t_en: Thank you so much for all you've done, {NICKNAME}. I never thought we'd drag the Express into a crisis again, much less one that might cause a rift between you and the IPC.

t_ch: 破裂？不至于不至于。况且这部分的主要责任也在我，怪我对下属管理不力。
t_en: A rift? I wouldn't go that far. Also, it's me who's mostly to blame — I didn't keep my subordinates in check.

t_ch: 希望你跟老板们多说我的好话！
t_en: Mind putting in some good words for me with your bosses?

t_ch: …哦？看来你已经有未来的职业规划咯？
t_en: Oh? Thinking about a career change?

t_ch: 没问题，我对你的评价可是很高的，{NICKNAME}~
t_en: Sure thing! I'll put in the best words I can, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 听说有人经历了十七轮面试…
t_en: Is it true the IPC requires you to go through <unbreak>17</unbreak> rounds of interviews?

t_ch: 十七轮？哦~我大概知道你说的是谁了。
t_en: <unbreak>17</unbreak>? Oh... I think I might know who you're talking about.

t_ch: 他那样的属于极端个例啦，正常的入职流程才不会拉那么长。
t_en: You could say he's a special case. For most people the interview process is much shorter.

t_ch: 能和你们这样闲聊真是太好了。
t_en: Hehe, I'm glad we're able to discuss some lighter topics.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你们这一路实在是辛苦，我给你们安排了歌德宾馆的房间，请务必在贝洛伯格好好恢复精神。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you all need some rest after everything you've been through. I've arranged rooms for you all at the Goethe Hotel.

t_ch: 托帕小姐，如果你需要一个舒适的地方落脚……
t_en: Miss Topaz, if you need somewhere comfortable to stay...

t_ch: 不劳烦你啦，我这就准备回去汇报工作了。
t_en: I appreciate the offer, but I need to head back and deliver my reports.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，布洛妮娅小姐——很高兴能跟你们建立联系。希望以后我们还有合作的机会…当然啦，合作方式肯定要换一种！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Miss Bronya, it's been great getting to know you both. I hope we can work together again in the future... only next time under better circumstances!

t_ch: 北方——大陆的最北方。
t_en: North... as far north as we can go.
...Huh?

t_ch: 败落不堪啊，这片城区…你们之前来过？
t_en: What a dismal sight. This district... Have you been here before?

t_ch: 对。我们在这里结束了一切……
t_en: Yes... This is where we put an end to it all...

t_ch: …然后又重启了一切。
t_en: ...And then began again.

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: That's right.

t_ch: 凯尔，久等了。我们现在可以过去了吗？
t_en: Kyle, sorry for the delay. Are we clear to proceed?

t_ch: 报告——前方的道路已经畅通了，大守护者大人。您和同伴可以放心北上了。
t_en: The path ahead is clear, Madam Guardian. You and your companions can proceed north without hindrance.

t_ch: 太好了。各位，前方的山路有些崎岖，请当心脚下。
t_en: Excellent. The mountain road ahead is a little treacherous, watch your step.

t_ch: …我们出发。
t_en: ...Let's go.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，还请你们先去歌德宾馆休息吧。我和托帕小姐还要聊聊后续的事宜。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, why don't you head over to the Goethe Hotel and relax for a bit? Miss Topaz and I still have a few matters to discuss.

t_ch: 这次没能让你们体会到节日的氛围，实在是遗憾…我保证，下次一定会尽力补偿各位。
t_en: I'm sorry you didn't get to experience the festival atmosphere this time around... I promise I'll make it up to you all next time.

t_ch: 话说回来，姬子是怎么知道这里发生的事的？
t_en: I've been wondering, how did you figure out what was happening here on Belobog, Himeko?

t_ch: 时刻关切列车组成员的状态，本就是领航员的分内责任。
t_en: Well, you know, keeping an eye on the crew is just another part of a navigator's job.

t_ch: （三月一直在期待的煦日节，看来这次也体验不上了啊……）
t_en: (March was looking forward to the Solwarm Festival for ages... Looks like she's missed her chance, this time...)

t_ch: （没想到这次又经历了…好困，该睡觉了……）
t_en: (I never thought this trip would be so... *yawn* ...tiring. Time for bed...)

t_ch: 既然是要收集民意，那肯定得兼顾上下层区居民的心情吧？
t_en: Alright, if we want to make an informed decision, we need to hear from both the Overworld and the Underworld, right?

t_ch: 那就先从上层区开始吧，出发。
t_en: Let's start with the Overworld.

t_ch: 哇，这么多人…没想到克里珀堡还有这么熙攘的时候。
t_en: Whoa, the fort's packed... I've never seen this many people here before.

t_ch: 不过感觉，稍微有点…吵耶。
t_en: Geez... I can barely even hear myself think!

t_ch: 居然真的是这里…怎么突然一个人都没有了？
t_en: Is this really the right place...? It's so quiet all of a sudden.

t_ch: 氛围完全不一样了，托帕真的在前面吗？
t_en: This atmosphere really feels strange... Are you sure Topaz is here?

t_ch: 哇…没想到，下层区居然还有这种地方？！
t_en: Wow... I didn't expect to find a place like this in the Underworld!

t_ch: 不知道托帕来这里是为了什么呢……
t_en: I wonder why Topaz would come to a place like this...

t_ch: 所以…我们现在该去哪呀，布洛妮娅？
t_en: So... where are we headed to now, Bronya?

t_ch: 这里是…这个地方，我们没来过耶！
t_en: ...What is this place? We've never been here before, right?

t_ch: 那是肯定，我们也是不久前才发现了这片区域。
t_en: Mm, we've only recently discovered this domain.

t_ch: 继续走吧，我想给各位看的东西就在前方。
t_en: Let's keep moving forward. What I want to show you is just ahead.

t_ch: 希露瓦！希露瓦！
t_en: Serval! Serval!

t_ch: 真的是她！是真人耶！
t_en: It's actually her! I can't believe it!

t_ch: 签名，请给我签名！
t_en: Could I pleeease get your autograph?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，快来快来！希露瓦好像要开嗓啦！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, quick! Serval's about to start singing!

t_ch: 嘘——大家安静一下！别着急，表演马上开始！
t_en: Woo! Thanks everyone! Hope you're ready for a great show! Let's get started!

t_ch: （周围的观众欢呼中）
t_en: (Audience cheering)

t_ch: （合唱）
t_en: (Singing along)

t_ch: 瞧一瞧，看一看嘞~
t_en: Come take a look!

t_ch: 歌蒂家的太阳薄饼~
t_en: Gertie's suncakes are fresh out of the oven⁓

t_ch: 妈妈！我要吃！
t_en: Mom, can I get one, pleeeease!?

t_ch: 队这么长啊…
t_en: The line's so long...

t_ch: 好多年没尝过了…
t_en: It's been years since I tried one...

t_ch: 这么便宜…卫生吗？
t_en: They're so cheap... Hope they're safe to eat.

t_ch: 歌蒂大婶——好久不见！
t_en: Aunt Gertie! It's been too long!

t_ch: 哎哟，这不是小三月吗——你们怎么来啦？我听人说，你们早就坐上大飞船，开到外太空去了呀？
t_en: Ah! Is that little March!? What brings you this way? I heard you left to go travel the galaxy?

t_ch: 哦，我知道了！你们肯定是听说了煦日节的事，特地回来体验节日气氛的，是吧？来来来，快来，我给你们烙一张热腾腾的太阳薄饼……
t_en: ...Oh, you're here for the Solwarm Festival, right? The atmosphere is like no other. Silly me, where are my manners? Grab yourselves a suncake each — get 'em while they're hot!

t_ch: 喂喂，前面那两个怎么插队啊？懂不懂先来后到啊？
t_en: Hey, that's not fair! Get to the back of the line. Ever heard of first come, first served?

t_ch: 别嚷嚷了！怎么，不认得这两位？要是没有{F#她们}{M#他们}，你怕是还蹲在矿坑里吃泥巴呢！
t_en: Tsk, show a little respect! If it weren't for these two, we'd be eating Geomarrow by now!

t_ch: 等待歌蒂烙饼中……
t_en: Waiting for Gertie's suncakes...

t_ch: 看看，看看！这是我今天烙得最满意的两张饼了！
t_en: Take a look at these beauties! These are the best suncakes I've made all day.

t_ch: 哇，好香啊！又焦又脆的感觉，太棒了！
t_en: Wow, they smell so good! They look so crispy and crunchy! I think I'm in love!

t_ch: 记得一定要趁热吃啊，凉了就没那个风味了——不好意思啊，后面的客人还在等着呢，下次有机会我再好好招待你俩……
t_en: Well, don't wait for them to get cold now. I'm sorry sweethearts, I'd best see to these other customers before their hunger gets the better of them. Thanks for stopping by!

t_ch: 现在开始介绍博物馆…
t_en: Okay kids, now we're going to learn about the museum...

t_ch: 大家听好啦…
t_en: Make sure you're all paying attention...

t_ch: 好饿……
t_en: I'm starving...

t_ch: 那边的铁卫，好酷哦！
t_en: Look — a Silvermane Guard! So cool!
*yawn*

t_ch: 那是佩拉吧？她又在带小孩子啦？
t_en: Is that Pela? Looks like she's teaching the kids again.

t_ch: 接下来，佩拉姐姐要开始介绍博物馆的历史背景了，大家要认真听哦。
t_en: Next, I'll be introducing the history of the museum. Everyone, listen carefully, okay?

t_ch: 贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆建立于<unbreak>600</unbreak>年前，起初是为了纪念初代大守护者阿丽萨……
t_en: Belobog's History and Culture Museum was founded <unbreak>600</unbreak> years ago, and was originally built to memorialize Supreme Guardian Alisa...

t_ch: 好饿哦…好想吃夕红鱼刺身……
t_en: Soooo hungry... I wish I could have a Redsunset Salmon right now...

t_ch: …咳，是为了纪念初代大守护者阿丽萨•兰德，还有……
t_en: *cough* It was built to memorialize Supreme Guardian Alisa Rand, and also...

t_ch: 你们知道嘛？上层区以后好像要开铁卫生活体验营，听说会很有趣哦？
t_en: Guess what — I heard there's gonna be a Silvermane adventure camp opening in the Overworld soon. It's gonna be super awesome.

t_ch: …还有在七百年前对抗侵略者的战争中牺牲的……
t_en: ...Also, those who sacrificed their lives in the war against the invaders <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago...

t_ch: 佩拉姐姐，我要告状！彼得他，要睡着啦！
t_en: Miss Pela! Peter is falling asleep!

t_ch: 够了，你们！从现在开始，禁止交头接耳——不想罚抄作业的话，就都给我好好听着！
t_en: Enough! From now on, no whispering allowed! If you don't want to have any homework later, you'd better start listening!

t_ch: 知、知道了，佩拉姐姐……
t_en: Sorry, Miss Pela...

t_ch: …这才像话嘛。咳，刚才说到哪里了？我们继续……
t_en: That's better. *cough* Now, where was I?

t_ch: 原来佩拉也有那么凶的一面啊……
t_en: I never knew Pela had a strict side...

t_ch: 列队，稍息！
t_en: Formation, at ease!

t_ch: 节庆期间，窃贼频出。
t_en: Thieves are rife during times of celebration.

t_ch: ——决不可放松警惕！
t_en: We must be vigilant!

t_ch: 收到，长官！
t_en: Yes, sir!

t_ch: …锵锵！戍卫官大人，瞧瞧谁回来了？
t_en: Ta-da! Guess who's back?

t_ch: 三月，还有{NICKNAME}？欢迎回到贝洛伯格。
t_en: It's you two! Welcome back to Belobog.

t_ch: 大守护者说过，会有神秘来宾前来参加煦日节的节庆活动——没想到她指的就是列车组的各位。
t_en: The Supreme Guardian mentioned there'd be mystery guests attending the Solwarm celebrations, but I didn't think it'd be the Express crew.

t_ch: 希望你们这一路上还算顺利，没有派出铁卫迎接各位是我的失职……
t_en: I hope you had a smooth journey. I should have dispatched Silvermane Guards to welcome you on arrival — my apologies.

t_ch: 不不不，你别道歉啦！我们也就是路过看看，不用搞得那么隆重。
t_en: It's fine! You really don't have to apologize. We're just passing through, it's no big deal.

t_ch: 明白，希望你们能享受节日的氛围。
t_en: Understood. I hope you both enjoy the festivities.

t_ch: …不过也要多加小心，城里的扒手就喜欢挑这种场合下手。请务必保管好私人财物。
t_en: Please make sure you keep your personal belongings safe — pickpockets tend to take advantage of events like this. We'll be performing additional patrols.

t_ch: 嗯！明白啦！那就不打扰你们工作了，回头见！
t_en: Got it! We'll get out of your way, then. Bye!

t_ch: 回见。
t_en: Bye for now.

t_ch: 罗希少爷在哪高就？
t_en: Master Rossy, may I inquire as to where you'll be employed next?

t_ch: 也就给我爸打打下手…
t_en: I'll be helping my father with his affairs...

t_ch: 哦，那诺尔伯特少爷…
t_en: Oh, and you, Master Norbert?

t_ch: 我？我还在改造期…
t_en: Me? Why, I'm still going through parol— a period of transformation...

t_ch: 等等，前面那不是…是华劳斯！他终于交到朋友啦？过去听听他们在说什么吧。
t_en: Hang on a sec, isn't that Wallace up ahead? Looks like he's finally made some friends! I wonder what they're talking about...

t_ch: …诺尔伯特，倒是跟大家说说，你怎么这么快就被放出来了啊？
t_en: ...Norbert, why don't you tell everyone how you were released from jail so quickly?

t_ch: 倒卖文物、假扮铁卫——这一条条的，听着都是大罪啊！
t_en: Selling cultural relics and impersonating Silvermane Guards are both heinous crimes!

t_ch: 呃…这不是多亏了我小叔嘛，你懂的……
t_en: Well... it was all thanks to my uncle, you know...

t_ch: 噢！想起来了，你小叔是那位诺伊尔大臣吧？交通部的领导？
t_en: Ahh, I remember! Your uncle is Minister Noel — he heads up the Department of Transportation, no?

t_ch: 呃…是，这你居然都知道？
t_en: Ahem... Yes, y—you knew that, too?

t_ch: 我去，兄弟，不是我说，你这回脸皮可够厚的！换成是我犯了事被扔进班房，肯定没脸叫我老爸过来捞人。
t_en: Haha, you're shameless, Norbert! Not to be rude, but if I were thrown in jail, I wouldn't possibly ask my father to bail me out.

t_ch: …不是，你以为我想啊？华劳斯，你是知道我的，我像那种仗势压人的主吗？
t_en: Do you think I wanted to? I'm not the type of person to abuse my status.

t_ch: 唔，这个…呃，我不好说……
t_en: Well, I... don't really know about that...

t_ch: …什么玩意，我在里面遭了这么多罪，现在出来了还要遭你们挤兑？你、你们算什么朋友！
t_en: Ugh, get off my back, will you? I had to deal with hardened criminals in jail — now that I'm free my supposed "friends" are ganging up on me!

t_ch: 呃…他们好像快吵起来了，咱们还是先走为上吧……
t_en: Looks like things are getting heated. I think we'd better get outta here.

t_ch: 怎么能这么想？！
t_en: How could you possibly consider this course of action!?

t_ch: 到底该怎么办……
t_en: What are we supposed to do...

t_ch: 你就只会中伤诽谤吗？
t_en: Is slander the only language you speak!?

t_ch: 不要歪解我的话！
t_en: Do not twist my words!

t_ch: 没希望了…没希望了…
t_en: We're doomed... doomed...

t_ch: 克里珀，庇佑我们……
t_en: Qlipoth, give us strength...

t_ch: 能不能都冷静点？！
t_en: Calm down, will you!?

t_ch: 拥护大守护者的决定！
t_en: I support the Supreme Guardian's decision!

t_ch: 托帕离开铆钉镇之后便不知了去向，她的部下随后涌入，占领了这里。
t_en: Topaz disappeared after leaving Rivet Town. In her absence, her subordinates flooded in and took over.

t_ch: 「公司」的士兵装备精良，和他们正面起冲突恐怕不太明智。你有什么办法吗，戍卫官？
t_en: The IPC soldiers are well-equipped — it wouldn't be wise to fight them head-on. Captain, what do you have in mind?

t_ch: 姬子小姐所言正是。为了能尽量隐蔽行踪，我们准备启用「潜爆机兵」。
t_en: You're right, Miss Himeko. We'll be using the Automaton Stealth Bomb to cover our tracks.

t_ch: 潜爆…机兵？
t_en: Automaton... Stealth Bomb?

t_ch: 具体的操作细节我会稍后解释，先前进吧。
t_en: I'll explain later. Let's get moving.

t_ch: 看，下面是公司的人！
t_en: Look — down there! It's the IPC!

t_ch: 各位，请尽量保持安静，不要惊动敌人。
t_en: Keep noise to a minimum — everyone. We don't want to alert the enemy to our presence.

t_ch: 该到「潜爆机兵」登场了。
t_en: It's time to deploy the Automaton Stealth Bomb.

t_ch: 准备好了吗，{NICKNAME}？操作「潜爆机兵」，排除公司的威胁吧。
t_en: Are you ready, {NICKNAME}? Use the Automaton Stealth Bomb to wipe out the IPC threats.

t_ch: 准备好了。
t_en: Ready.

t_ch: 记住，要尽量保证机兵不被敌人发现——行动越隐蔽，成功率越高。
t_en: Remember, try to avoid being noticed. Remaining hidden will increase our chances of success.

t_ch: 糟糕，被他们识破了……
t_en: Oh no, they saw right through it...

t_ch: 没关系，再试一次。我们准备了很多备用的「潜爆机兵」。
t_en: It's alright — we prepared many backups. Try again.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你要去哪？回来回来！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, where are you going? Come back!

t_ch: 我明白，你心中战斗的欲望正在高涨…但这次还是用「潜爆机兵」解决问题吧。
t_en: I know that your urge to fight is overwhelming... but let's use the Automaton Stealth Bomb this time.

t_ch: 哇！「嘭」的一下就把他们炸晕了，效率真高！
t_en: Wow! We wiped them all out in a single explosion! That's what you call efficiency!

t_ch: 若非迫不得已，我们也不想用这种手段。
t_en: It's a shame we had to resort to this method, but we had no choice.

t_ch: 你们铁卫还真是正直……
t_en: You Silvermane Guards sure are a righteous bunch...

t_ch: 那种制式的机器人，我以前见过。是公司执行外勤的「小组长」。
t_en: I've seen standard-issue robots like these before. It's an IPC field Team Leader.

t_ch: 嗯，它的外观和「灰熊」类似，但武器更先进，装甲也更坚硬。
t_en: Correct — the exterior is similar to the Grizzly, but its weapons and armor are more advanced.

t_ch: 希望「潜爆机兵」的攻击能对它奏效。{NICKNAME}，准备好了就告诉我吧。
t_en: Hopefully, the Automaton Stealth Bomb will still be effective. {NICKNAME}, let me know when you're ready.

t_ch: 好耶！这下道路都畅通啦。
t_en: Woohoo! The coast is clear!

t_ch: 实话说，我也没想到威力会这么大…还好一切顺利。
t_en: To be honest, I didn't think it would be so powerful... Good thing everything went according to plan.

t_ch: 托帕就是顺着这条路离开了铆钉镇。
t_en: This is the road that Topaz took when she left Rivet Town.

t_ch: 走吧，我们去找到她，告诉她布洛妮娅的决定。
t_en: Let's find her and tell her about Bronya's decision.

t_ch: 如果有必要的话…软硬兼施！
t_en: This time, the gloves are off!

t_ch: 托帕来到这里，是为了找什么东西吗？
t_en: Why would Topaz come here? Is she looking for something?

t_ch: 有可能。这个世界的地下藏着不少秘密。
t_en: It's possible. Many mysteries lurk beneath the surface of this world.

t_ch: 但公司的手段从来都不止「交易」一种，这时就该我这类人出马了，对不？怎么样，千载难逢的机会，考虑一下吧，说不定能将功补过哦。
t_en: But as we both know, the IPC does more than just "business deals" — guess it's my time to shine. So, how about it? This is a rare opportunity. Consider it. You never know, you might even be able to make up for your blunder on Jarilo-VI.

t_ch: …我晚点答复你。
t_en: ...I'll get back to you later.

t_ch: …真是好一顿折腾啊。
t_en: ...Seems it's just one wild ride after another...

t_ch: 但愿我这次的判断没出问题……
t_en: ...but I hope I made the right decision this time...

t_ch: 这雪，还挺漂亮的。
t_en: This snow is mesmerizing...

t_ch: 收到，长官！
t_en: Yes, sir!

t_ch: 娜塔莎医生，需要我的帮助吗？
t_en: Need any help, Dr. Natasha?

t_ch: 你来得正好，{NICKNAME}，我有些事想麻烦你。
t_en: You came at a good time, {NICKNAME}, there is something you can help me with.

t_ch: 根据病历簿上的记录，有一些病人最近没来复诊，说实话我有些担心——虽然类似的情况时有发生，但从来没有像最近这么频繁。
t_en: According to my patient records, there are a few that haven't come for their follow-up visits. It happens from time to time, but never as regularly as this. I'm a bit concerned...

t_ch: …你听说过「疑难重症患者互助会」吗？
t_en: Have you heard of the Complex Illness Mutual Aid Society?

t_ch: 听上去像是个公益组织。
t_en: Sounds like a charitable organization.

t_ch: 听上去很可疑…
t_en: Sounds suspicious...

t_ch: 嗯，我也有同感。
t_en: Mm, I agree.

t_ch: 通常而论，在类似的互助会上，患者之间能够交流经历、互相支持鼓励，努力战胜疾病对各自的负面影响…但眼下这个「互助会」恐怕没那么单纯。
t_en: Generally speaking, mutual aid societies like these allow patients to share their experiences and support each other. It's a way of countering all the negative emotional effects of being ill... But there might be more to this society than that.

t_ch: 我的有些患者，自从参加了「疑难重症患者互助会」之后便开始拒绝治疗——很难不让人浮想联翩。
t_en: I've got a few patients who've started refusing treatment since joining the society... It certainly makes you wonder.

t_ch: 更可疑的是，不管我再怎么追问，始终没人愿意告诉我和这个「互助会」相关的细节…所以我才想向你求助。
t_en: The most suspicious part is that no matter how much I ask, no one's ever willing to tell me anything about it... and that's where you might be able to help.

t_ch: 我明白了，你是想让我去卧底。
t_en: Got it, you want me to go undercover.

t_ch: 听着有些凶险，但你要做的其实很简单。我希望你能帮我调查一下这个所谓的「互助会」，看看他们到底在鼓捣什么。
t_en: ...It sounds dangerous, but what I want you to do is actually very simple. Investigate this so-called "Mutual Aid Society" and find out what exactly they're up to.

t_ch: 我要得什么病才能混进去？
t_en: Do I need to be ill before I can make inquiries?

t_ch: …倒不需要你做到这种地步。我只是希望你能假装患者，帮我调查一下这个所谓的「互助会」，看看他们到底在鼓捣什么。
t_en: ...That won't be necessary, but it will help if you pretend to be a patient. Help me investigate this so-called "Mutual Aid Society" and find out exactly what they're up to.

t_ch: 我该怎么做？
t_en: What should I do?

t_ch: 我希望你能假装患者，帮我调查一下这个所谓的「互助会」，看看他们到底在鼓捣什么。
t_en: Hmm... Pretend to be a patient. Help me investigate this so-called "Mutual Aid Society" and find out exactly what they're up to.

t_ch: 如果「互助会」是在瞒着我这个主治医生，做些可能危害到患者的事…那我坚决不能坐视不理。
t_en: If I find out they're preventing me from doing my job... or if they're bringing harm to my patients... I won't just sit on the sidelines.

t_ch: 我可以把这个任务交给你吗，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: Can I entrust you with this task, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 没问题，包在我身上。
t_en: No problem, leave it with me.

t_ch: 你看我有拒绝过你吗？
t_en: Have I ever refused you?

t_ch: 真是一如既往地贴心呢，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: You're as considerate as always, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我这小小的诊所，每天都要面对大量前来问诊的病患…多亏有你帮我分担压力，我才能静下心来专心照料病人。
t_en: This little clinic has huge numbers of patients to deal with every day... If I didn't have you here to help me out, I wouldn't be able to focus on giving them quality care.

t_ch: 那就拜托你了，{NICKNAME}。等你回来以后，我再好好感谢你。
t_en: Good luck, {NICKNAME}. I'll be sure to thank you properly when you're back.

t_ch: 你…我在诊所里见过你，你不是那个帮娜塔莎跑腿的家伙吗？
t_en: You... I've seen you at the clinic. You run errands for Natasha, right?

t_ch: 卧底计划这么快就失败了…
t_en: Well, there goes my cover...

t_ch: …卧什么底，你在跟谁说话呢？
t_en: ...Your cover? What are you talking about?

t_ch: 呃…娜塔莎是谁？
t_en: Uh... Who's Natasha?

t_ch: …少装蒜了，我的眼神不会骗人。
t_en: ...Don't play dumb. I might be sick but my eyes still work.

t_ch: 我猜猜，是娜塔莎让你来劝我去复诊的？
t_en: *sigh* Let me guess, Natasha wants you to persuade me to go back for a follow-up visit, right?

t_ch: 哎…还有什么意义呢？我都已经得了绝症了 ，完蛋了，这辈子就这样了吧。
t_en: *sigh* ...What's the use? I'm terminal. Life'll be over before I know it.

t_ch: 这是娜塔莎的诊断结果？
t_en: Was that Natasha's diagnosis?

t_ch: 当然不是，是我自己从书上看到的。
t_en: Of course not, I read it in a book.

t_ch: 我每看到一个书上描述的病症，就感觉自己的情况完全符合…这书才翻到一半，我就已经和十几种不治之症对上号了。
t_en: Every book I read has descriptions that match my symptoms exactly... I can diagnose myself with a dozen incurable diseases before I get halfway through.

t_ch: 像我这种情况，就算去复诊也只是浪费时间和金钱…哦，还浪费了医生开的药。
t_en: For people in my situation, a follow-up visit is just a waste of time and money... not to mention medicine.

t_ch: 真的假的？
t_en: Are you serious?

t_ch: 不是因为参加「互助会」吗？
t_en: Soooo nothing to do with you joining the mutual aid society then?

t_ch: 互助会？什么互助会？
t_en: Mutual aid what now? What are you talking about?

t_ch: …不和你扯了，其实我就是没钱继续看病了。虽然娜塔莎医生说我可以一直赊账，等以后有机会再还…但我这人就是脸皮薄。
t_en: ...What else is there to say. Natasha was seeing me free of charge. She said I could keep a tab running for as long as I liked, then pay it back when I had the opportunity... I guess I'm too proud.

t_ch: 钱是小事，命是…
t_en: Money's one thing, but life...

t_ch: 这点道理还用你说吗？别劝我了，我已经想清楚了……
t_en: You think I need you to tell me? Don't try and persuade me, I've already thought this through...

t_ch: 面子比命重要，我懂你的意思。
t_en: Appearances are more important than life, I feel you.

t_ch: …你在讽刺我吗？放弃吧，激将这招对我没用。
t_en: ...Are you making fun of me? Have at it, you won't change my mind.

t_ch: 哎…我之前去瓦赫医生那里看病都是不用花钱的。虽然娜塔莎人也挺好，但果然在我心里，瓦赫医生才是第一位的……
t_en: *sigh* ...Dr. Vache never charged me anything either. Natasha's great, but Vache was always my number one...

t_ch: 谁是瓦赫医生？
t_en: Who's Dr. Vache?

t_ch: 瓦赫医生就是娜塔莎的哥哥啊，以前这俩人都在铆钉镇行医。
t_en: Natasha's brother? They both used to practice medicine back in Rivet Town.

t_ch: 听说瓦赫医生是上层区医学院的高材生，贝洛伯格的大医院都抢着要他。也不知道他为什么会跑到这穷酸的下层…可能就是纯粹的心善吧？
t_en: I heard he was a top student from the Overworld medical academy — Belobog's main hospital wanted him. I've no idea what convinced him to set up down here... a kind heart, maybe.

t_ch: 不收钱的医生，我信不过…
t_en: Free medical care? I don't believe it...

t_ch: 你在说啥？瓦赫医生是「穷人们的医生」，他总是给我们分发免费的药物，还会仔细询问我们吃药之后的感受。
t_en: What do you mean? Dr. Vache was a people's doctor — he handed out free medicine. He even followed up with detailed questions on how we felt.

t_ch: 下层区一直都缺乏药物资源，据说这些药都是瓦赫医生自己研制出来的。
t_en: The Underworld is always short on medical resources — they say that Dr. Vache made all his own medicine himself.

t_ch: 为什么不继续去找瓦赫医生？
t_en: Why don't you go to him anymore?

t_ch: 你以为我不想吗？我听人说…瓦赫医生不在人世了。
t_en: You think I don't want to? I heard that he... passed away.

t_ch: 那个…接下来的话，我只小声告诉你，你可不要随便外传……
t_en: Uh... I should keep my voice down. Don't go telling anyone else about this next bit...

t_ch: 我保证不说出去。
t_en: I won't.

t_ch: 我听人说，瓦赫医生离世跟他妹妹娜塔莎有关。说是那个女人嫉妒瓦赫医生抢走了自己的病人，所以才……
t_en: I heard that Natasha had something to do with Dr. Vache's death. They say she was jealous that her brother had stolen her patients, so she...

t_ch: 这说法你信吗？
t_en: And you believe that?

t_ch: 哎呀，我也只是道听途说…我也不信娜塔莎医生能干出这种事。
t_en: Agh, it's just something people were saying... I don't think Dr. Natasha could do something like that.

t_ch: 好像有这个可能性…
t_en: It's not impossible...

t_ch: …等会儿，只是个坊间传闻而已，你怎么就信了？
t_en: ...Hey, it's just a rumor. Don't take my word for it.

t_ch: 哎…算了，不跟你多说了。既然娜塔莎医生特地派你来劝我，那我就姑且听她一句吧。
t_en: *sigh* Forget I said anything. Since Dr. Natasha sent you over specially, I'll do her a favor.

t_ch: 一会儿我就去诊所再复查一次，再见……
t_en: I'll go to the clinic for my follow-up a little later. Bye for now...

t_ch: （这个患者，因为经济拮据的原因没有去复诊……）
t_en: (Avoiding a follow-up visit due to money issues...)

t_ch: （再找另一个患者打听一下吧……）
t_en: (I should find another patient to talk to...)

t_ch: …你说什么？大声点儿！我耳朵不好，听不清楚。
t_en: ...What was that? You'll have to be louder! My ears aren't what they used to be — everything's muffled.

t_ch: 请问你为什么不去复诊？
t_en: I wanted to ask why you're not going for your follow-up visit.

t_ch: 啊？为什么不去复诊？你是要问我这个吗？
t_en: Hah? Follow-up visit? Is that what you're asking about?

t_ch: 哎，娜塔莎是个好姑娘，每次给我检查都特别认真细致……
t_en: *sigh* Natasha's a wonderful girl. She always gives me a thorough checkup...

t_ch: 不过…你看我这一把年纪，早就经不起折腾了。就算能治好病，也活不长了。这多浪费啊……
t_en: But at this age, it's rather a lot to bear. Even if they could cure me, I'm sure I wouldn't be around for long. I don't want to waste anyone's time...

t_ch: 为了健康考虑，哪有浪费一说？
t_en: There's no time wasted where health is concerned, right?

t_ch: 这话是说给年轻人听的吧？我现在的状况，早就不奢求什么身体健康了……
t_en: Not for youngsters like yourself. When you get to my time of life, good health is a bit of a luxury I'm afraid.

t_ch: 听听娜塔莎医生怎么说吧。
t_en: You should listen to Dr. Natasha.

t_ch: 那姑娘一直劝我按时吃药，我知道她是一片好心……
t_en: That girl's always trying to get me to take my medicine on time. *sigh* I know her heart's in the right place...

t_ch: 其实我家的老东西患了跟我一样的病，但他光让我吃药，自己却什么都不吃。
t_en: My husband had the same illness as me, but he never touched the medicine. He wanted to make sure I had enough.

t_ch: 后来有一天，老东西说是要去矿区办事儿，结果就再也没出来……
t_en: Then one day, he said he needed to take care of some things at the mine. He never came back...

t_ch: 他以前一直说，想走在我前面…哎，这老东西，真是自私……
t_en: He was forever saying he wanted to go before I did. Hehe... Selfish old fool...

t_ch: 我唯一挂念的老伴儿都不在了，还有谁会在乎我活多久呢？
t_en: I miss him. Now that he's gone, who cares how long I stick around?

t_ch: 你老伴的在天之灵会在乎。
t_en: I'm sure he's up there caring right now.

t_ch: …他真会这么想？他难道不会希望我早点儿去陪他吗……
t_en: ...Do you think so? Wouldn't he want me to hurry up and join him...?

t_ch: …哎，现在的年轻人，可真难打发……
t_en: Hmph... Kids today don't give up easily.

t_ch: 娜塔莎医生在乎。
t_en: Dr. Natasha cares.

t_ch: …你说的倒也有几分道理。哎，那小姑娘一直都对我尽心尽力，但我一直觉得自己是她的负担……
t_en: ...You're not wrong, I suppose. That girl certainly spares me no effort, but I always feel like I'm a burden to her...

t_ch: 我在乎。
t_en: I care.

t_ch: …你还真是难打发啊，不过…你说话倒也挺中听的。
t_en: You don't give up easily, do you? Still... you're full of kind words.

t_ch: 行吧，那我就听你一回，再去娜塔莎医生那儿复诊一次……
t_en: Okay, you win. It's off for another follow-up visit with Dr. Natasha, I suppose...

t_ch: 嗯？你又想说什么？我耳朵不太好使……
t_en: Mm? Did you want to say something else? My ears are terrible...

t_ch: 您听说过「互助会」吗？
t_en: Have you heard of the mutual aid society?

t_ch: 「互助」什么？
t_en: Virtual who?

t_ch: 「互助会」！
t_en: Mutual aid society!

t_ch: 听到了听到了…小声点儿！没有，我才没听说过什么「互助会」……
t_en: Alright, I heard you! Do you have to shout? ...No, I've never heard of any mutual aid society...

t_ch: （老人是因为自己老伴的原因没有去复诊……）
t_en: (Avoiding a follow-up visit due to her husband...)

t_ch: （再找另一个患者打听一下吧……）
t_en: (I should find another patient to talk to...)

t_ch: 你问我为什么不去复诊…你也是患者吗，还是说你是娜塔莎医生派来的？
t_en: Why haven't I gone for my follow-up visit... Are you a patient too, or did Dr. Natasha send you over?

t_ch: 为什么会这么认为？
t_en: What makes you say that?

t_ch: 不然呢，还有谁会关心我们这种人？
t_en: Who else would care about people like me?

t_ch: 你挺敏锐的嘛。
t_en: You're pretty sharp.

t_ch: 嚯，那你就快回去吧，帮我谢谢娜塔莎医生的关心。
t_en: Hah, you should head on back then. Thank Dr. Natasha for her concern.

t_ch: 我现在不需要娜塔莎医生的帮助，让她省点心也省点药，去救助其他人吧。
t_en: I don't need Dr. Natasha's help now. Tell her to save her worries and medicine for other people.

t_ch: 你和娜塔莎医生有过节？
t_en: Did you have a dispute with her?
